ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic pizza class (FOODON_00002572). No specific class exists for rendang-topped or lion's mane mushroom pizza. Closest parent match used; curry rendang topping and lion's mane mushroom are not captured in the ontology.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00KG covers pizza as a generic category. No specific facet exists for rendang curry topping or lion's mane mushroom in FoodEx2 as of 2024. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for pizza or rendang pizza dish. Omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

main_ingredients: pizza dough (wheat flour, yeast, water, salt)
rendang curry sauce (coconut milk, lemongrass, galangal, turmeric, chilli, kaffir lime leaf)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is 2 slices (approximately 150 g), representing about one quarter of a standard 10-inch hand-stretched pizza.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza Curry Rendang Lion Mane — Singapore HPB Foodo Database (G00010743)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database record G00010743 with confidence 0.95. All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken verbatim from the input record. Saturated fat is estimated from rendang coconut milk composition using USDA FoodData Central (coconut milk, canned, NDB 12117) as a supplementary reference for fatty acid breakdown, as the HPB record does not disaggregate fat fractions.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database — Pizza, cheese (reference food type)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Pizza Curry Rendang with Lion's Mane Mushroom. GI of 68 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cheese pizza on a refined wheat base (GI ~60–80, median ~68; Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008, Table entry for pizza, cheese). The rendang sauce (coconut milk, spices) and lion's mane mushroom topping do not substantially alter the glycaemic response relative to the wheat dough base. High sugar content (15.75 g/100g) from the rendang sauce may elevate the effective GI slightly above a plain cheese pizza baseline. GL per serving calculated as: GI × available carbohydrate per serving / 100 = 68 × (32.38 × 1.50) / 100 = 68 × 48.57 / 100 ≈ 33.1.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (15.75 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (1.7 g/100g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium at 130 mg/100g accumulates to approximately 195 mg per 150 g serving; saturated fat from coconut milk rendang sauce promotes arterial stiffness.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit intake to one slice, request reduced coconut milk in the rendang sauce to lower saturated fat, and pair with a high-fibre vegetable side to blunt the insulin-sodium retention response.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat pizza base delivers 32.38 g carbohydrate/100g with a medium-high GI (~68) and high sugar content (15.75 g/100g) against only 1.7 g/100g dietary fibre, producing a glycaemic load of approximately 33 per 150 g serving — well above the low-GL threshold of 10.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with type 2 diabetes should restrict to one slice, request a wholemeal or cauliflower crust to increase fibre and reduce GI, retain the lion's mane mushroom topping for its beta-glucan-mediated blood glucose regulation benefit, and monitor postprandial glucose closely.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat of approximately 4.8 g/100g from coconut milk in the rendang sauce raises LDL cholesterol risk; cholesterol is low at 3 mg/100g; lion's mane mushroom beta-glucans may offer a modest cardioprotective benefit.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit portion to one slice to keep saturated fat intake below 5 g per eating occasion, request reduced coconut milk preparation, and note that the lion's mane mushroom topping provides beta-glucans that may modestly support lipid profile management.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Chilli (nightshade) in rendang sauce may exacerbate joint symptoms in nightshade-sensitive individuals; high sugar (15.75 g/100g) promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; turmeric (curcumin) and lemongrass in rendang are potent anti-inflammatory agents; lion's mane beta-glucans have immune-modulating properties.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis who are nightshade-sensitive should request a chilli-free rendang preparation, limit portion to one slice to minimise saturated fat and sugar-driven inflammation, and note that turmeric and lion's mane mushroom in this dish provide anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pizza Curry Rendang with Lion's Mane Mushroom is a Singaporean fusion dish that combines a hand-stretched, fermented wheat pizza base with a rich rendang curry sauce and lion's mane mushroom (Hericium erinaceus) as the primary topping. The pizza dough is made from scratch — mixed, fermented, and hand-stretched — producing a chewy, slightly tangy base with a crisp underside when baked. The rendang sauce is a slow-cooked Malay-Indonesian curry reduction made from coconut milk, lemongrass, galangal, turmeric, kaffir lime leaf, and chilli, delivering a deeply aromatic, mildly spiced, and slightly sweet flavour profile. Lion's mane mushroom contributes a meaty, seafood-like texture and a mild umami flavour, while also providing beta-glucans with documented immune-modulating and neuroprotective properties. Per 100 g, the dish provides 204 kcal, 32.38 g carbohydrate, 7.53 g fat (predominantly saturated from coconut milk, estimated ~4.8 g/100g), 1.84 g protein, 15.75 g sugar, and 1.7 g dietary fibre. The high sugar and refined carbohydrate content relative to fibre produces a medium-high glycaemic index (~68) and a high glycaemic load per serving (~33), making this dish a concern for individuals managing type 2 diabetes or hypertension. Sodium is moderate at 130 mg/100g. The dish is halal-suitable and vegetarian. It is typically served at lunch or dinner in Singapore's fusion restaurant scene and is culturally positioned as a creative local-Western hybrid celebrating Malay culinary heritage with functional mushroom ingredients.
common_user_queries: Is pizza curry rendang suitable for diabetics?
What is lion's mane mushroom pizza?
Lion's mane mushroom health benefits on pizza
Is pizza curry rendang high in sugar?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database record G00010743 with a stated confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central coconut milk data as a supplementary reference. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (cheese pizza, refined wheat base). All other values are preserved verbatim from the canonical HPB source.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin profile, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this specific fusion pizza preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo database (G00010743) or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 2. saturated_fat_g (4.8 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central coconut milk (canned, NDB 12117) proportional contribution to the rendang sauce, as the HPB record does not disaggregate fat fractions; this is an approximation and should be verified against a direct laboratory analysis. 3. GI of 68 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (cheese pizza, refined wheat base); no direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. 4. The input record classified primary_cuisine as 'Japanese', which is not supported by the dish composition (rendang is Malay-Indonesian; pizza is Italian-Western; the dish is a Singaporean fusion creation). This has been corrected to 'Singaporean Fusion' with region_of_origin set to 'Singapore'. Multilingual naming follows the Singaporean dish rule (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). 5. Protein_g (1.84), fat_g (7.53), and carbohydrate_g (32.38) are all non-zero; no zero-macro justification is required. 6. Ontology match types corrected from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' for both FoodOn and FoodEx2 to conform to the allowed values: exact, closest_parent, none.
